While I understand the sentiment behind "The enemy of my enemy is my friend" as a strategic or tactical alignment, I do not automatically equate opposition to a shared adversary with full ideological alignment or trust. On personal freedom issues—where I take a strongly left-wing stance—I may find common cause with certain groups in opposing authoritarian overreach, even if they are otherwise ideologically opposed to me. Similarly, on economic issues—where I am strongly right-wing—I might align with others against excessive government intervention in markets.

However, this alignment is situational and issue-specific, not a blanket endorsement of the principle. I value principle over convenience, so I don’t fully embrace the idea as an absolute rule. Therefore, I agree it can be true in practice, but not universally—hence, a 3: "agree," but with reservations.
